Director - ADMINISTRATION AND COMPLIANCE MANAGER

Direct

Blakes Hotel in London remains closed with future uncertain 

 

 

The future of Blakes Hotel in London’s South Kensington is uncertain, as the property remains closed for business.

The five-star hotel closed on May 31, 2022, for a renovation and was scheduled to reopen on July 31, 2023. However, its doors remain shut two years later.

The hotel’s website is still active, but there is a note on it which reads ‘temporarily closed’. Emails cannot be delivered to the hotel and its phone number rings through to a standard O2 voicemail message.

 

Blakes was previously a member of Design Hotels, a 300+ strong collection of design-led properties around the world. However, a spokesperson confirmed to Boutique Hotelier that the hotel has not been a part of Design Hotels since 2022.

An FAQ section on Blakes Hotel’s website says the property is ‘proudly owned by a private independent entity’.

Boutique Hotelier understands this to be Blakes Management Limited. Companies House filings show th

Blakes Hotel

Blakes Hotel is a 5-star hotel in London, and considered one of the world's first boutique hotels. It is at 33 Roland Gardens in South Kensington. It was established by London hotelier and designer Anouska Hempel, and contains eclectic artifacts collected by Hempel during her international travels. Known for protecting its clients' privacy from the paparazzi, it often accommodates celebrities. Regular guests have included Princess Margaret; Sarah Ferguson; actresses Gwyneth Paltrow and Lindsay Lohan; and supermodel Kate Moss..

The hotel is also home to a Mediterranean-style restaurant and an exclusive lounge named Blakes Below, which debuted in 2016.

The hotel was mentioned as a plot point in Sofia Coppola's 2020 film On the Rocks.

The hotel has been closed since May 31, 2022.

## The Athenaeum Hotel & Residences appoints new general manager 28 July 2025

Hospitality veteran, Edward Bracken joins the Mayfair-based property

Share This Article

The Athenaeum Hotel & Residences is delighted to announce the appointment of its new General Manager, Edward Bracken marking a new era for the award-winning hotel.With over 20 years of hospitality experience, Edward brings a wealth of knowledge and a proven track record of delivering guest-centric results. His vision is to further enhance the hotel’s reputation by fostering an empowered team and offering exceptional, personalised guest experiences.Originally from Limerick, Edward studied at the Shannon College of Hotel Management and has since built an impressive career across a number of renowned London Hotels including The Dixon, Tower Bridge, Lost Property St. Paul’s London, Marrio

Meet the one and only Axel Krueger, our General Manager. We caught up with Axel to find out about what he loves about working at art’otel London Hoxton, how he likes to spend his time out of work, and much more! With a bold and approachable personality, Axel encapsulates the spirit of art’otel and is the perfect person for the job. # Inside the Londoner, Leicester Square

02.12.2021

Laura Osborne and Mark Hilton spoke to Charles Oak, Hotel Director of the Londoner, a new super-boutique hotel in the heart of the capital. From opening a new 16-storey hotel in uncertain times, through to the importance of its art collection and cultural contribution, Charles shares the highs and lows of bringing this new iconic destination to the capital.

Charlotte Weatherall on being part of the LGBTQ+ community in the luxury sector | The Walpole --

###### Inclusive Luxury

###### 28th June 2023

Walpole: What does your role entail, and how long have you been doing it?

Charlotte Weatherall: As Director of Sales & Marketing for Corinthia London, I am responsible for the ensuring the hotel is positioned to achieve the highest revenue performance possible. I have worked in luxury hospitality for over 20 years and been at Corinthia London for just over a year.

What’s your career background and what makes you passionate about what you do?

My career in hospitality started with Starwood Hotels & Resorts completing their management training programme ‘Vita Futura’. From there I moved into sales and marketing, and have been fortunate to hold a number of roles in both disciplines in the UK and UAE with brands such as Westin, Rocco Forte, Mandarin Oriental, Langham and, now, Corinthia.

I am fortunate enough to work with fantastically talente

experience.title: Managing Director
experience.description: I am responsible for the overall portfolio performance, strategic development, and owner relations of Switzerland’s largest hospitality investment, the Bürgenstock Hotels AG. With experience spanning several continents, I have an accomplished track record in luxury, upscale and select service hotels, having operated through some of the most demanding business scenarios including economic downturns, uprisings, pandemics, and embargoes. I am known to be strategically focused, financially disciplined, and result-oriented with a strong track record of leadership and innovation. I am passionate about delivering benchmark setting performances for the owners through astute management and strategies. I believe first and foremost in the excellence of service, and passion in everything we do being the key to our success. With a thorough background in Food & Beverage, substantial experience in Rooms Division and Sales, Marketing as well as Controlling, I have a holistic understanding of operations. I also possess thorough knowledge of Unions and negotiation tactics as well as handling challenging ownership situations for various properties. I have successfully launched several new food and beverage concepts that were later replicated in many other hotels across many countries. Recent accolades include being selected as Newcomer of the Year 2024 by the Karl Wild Hotel Rating in Switzerland and the inclusion in the General Manager’s Power List Middle East 2023, a sign of excellence for industry leaders who have excelled and set industry leading benchmarks. He also successfully executed Switzerland’s largest ever political conference in June of 2024, Peace for Ukraine, with over 100 delegations in attendance and almost 60 Heads of State. As of October 2024, Chris is also member of the board of Swiss Deluxe Hotels, covering 38 of Switzerland’s most prolific and luxurious hotels and resorts. Chris is also a member of the European Hotel Managers Association (EHMA) since 2024.

linkedin_profile.headline: Director Of Operations at The Twenty Two
linkedin_profile.about:
I've studied Catering, Hotel and Restaurant Management in my native France whilst beginning my waiting career in hotels and restaurants across central France. Following a move to the UK in 2007, i've joined Pearl, the then Chancery Court hotel and fine dining restaurant. From there, I've joined Lutyens on Fleet Street (the Prescott & Conran venue) as Assistant Manager, overseeing a large team across two restaurants and two bars. Gaining valuable experience as acting General Manager for a portion of my time with the company, I was later promoted to Private Club & Private Dining Manager. Where I exclusively managed the four private dining rooms and all-day members club. In 2015, I have joined as General Manager at The Ninth restaurant by the celebrity chef Jun Tanaka. The Ninth is an award winning 1 Michelin start restaurant and 3 AA rosettes. Located on Charlotte street in the heart of London’s buzzy Fitzrovia neighbourhood, the contemporary, yet warm and inviting 90-seater restaurant offers up simple but refined French Mediterranean-style dishes, all made for sharing. From 2018, I've joined the Frenchie Group team in London as a General Manager of its restaurant in Covent Garden. SInce December 2019, I am been promoted to Operations Director to FHG and taking care of all operations between France, UK and all other projects within the group.

Total Experience: 13 years and 2 months

Scotland hotels plan revealed by Dalata chief Dermot Crowley | The Herald

Exclusive

# Scotland hotels plan revealed by Dalata chief Dermot Crowley

Exclusive by Ian McConnell Group Business Editor

Share

0 Comments

Dermot Crowley underlined expansion ambitions (Image: Dalata Hotel Group)

This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald.

Ireland’s Dalata Hotel Group has underlined its ambitions for further expansion in Edinburgh, while highlighting the strong performance of its two properties in Glasgow.

Chief executive Dermot Crowley told The Herald in an interview yesterday: “We love Edinburgh. We would love more hotels in Edinburgh. We are always actively looking but it is a very hard one to get into.”

Dalata, which has 53 hotels, last year bought a building in St Andrew Square for its first property in Edinburgh.

Profile

Dr. Aron Hari Harilela

Chairman & CEOHarilela Hotels Ltd

Dr. Aron Harilela is CEO and Chairman of the Harilela Group, a Hong Kong based private company that has extensive experience in hotel development and management and operates hotels in Hong Kong, Mainland China and other parts of the world. They started its own brand, The Hari London in 2016 and Hong Kong in 2020. In 2014, he was appointed a Justice of the Peace and in 2019 was awarded the Bronze Bauhinia Star (BBS) by the HKSAR Government for his contributions to the community and hotel industry. Aron graduated with an honors Bachelor degree in Law and Politics, and a PhD in Political Philosophy from University of Hull, England.

# How prominent Hong Kong hotelier Dr Aron Harilela is navigating the pandemic

It’s a trying time for the hospitality and tourism industry but Dr Aron Harilela is taking bold steps, including opening The Hari Hong Kong late last year.

By The Peak Team/ 15 Jun 2021

Dr Aron Harilela

Business Leaders, Hospitality, Tourism, Travel

##### Share this article

How big are the Harilelas? They have their own Wikipedia page. One of the most prominent families in Hong Kong, the Harilelas own and operate The Harilela Group, which focuses on hotel development and management. Last year, in December during the middle of a pandemic, the Group opened the doors to its latest property, The Hari Hong Kong.

The Peak speaks to chairperson and CEO of The Harilela Group, Dr Aron Harilela, about this bold move a

The Hari, London
[![Hospitality Net](https://www.hospitalitynet.org/HN-icon.svg)![Hospitality Net]()](https://www.hospitalitynet.org/)

# The Hari, London
![The Hari, London](https://www.hospitalitynet.org/picture/153165163/the-hari-london.jpg?t=1705567546)The Hari in London opened its doors in August 2016. The hotel is the vision of Dr. Aron Harilela, Chairman and CEO of Harilela Hotels Ltd, and the first hotel to open under the ‘The Hari’ brand. A second hotel opened in 2020 in Hong Kong. The Harilela Group was established in 1959 and is still wholly owned by the Harilela family. The Harilela Group is the parent company of Harilela Hotels which owns 15 properties across Hong Kong, China, the Far East, Europe, and the US.

# Company Information

Established in 1959 and still wholly owned by the Harilela family, The Harilela Group is a Hong Kong based private company with extensive experience in hotel development and management. The Group currently owns and operates some 15 properties across Hong Kong, China, the Far East, Europe and the US. Our latest is The Hari Hong Kong which opened in late 2020

The Group’s success in generating steady returns for its shareholder is based on ensuring its hotels and hospitality related properties remain the preferred choice of discerning guests. Such a steady focus on customer satisfaction has paved the way for the acquisition of several quality brands across virtually every segment of the international hotel and leisure market.

Diversification into hotels began with the acquisition of Hong Kong’s Imperial Hotel in 1961. Half a century on, The Group has successfully spread its wings into t

Hari Harilela

Hari Naroomal Harilela, GBM GBS OBE JP(Chinese: 夏利萊; Jyutping: haa6 lei6 loi4, 10 August 1922 – 29 December 2014) was a Hong Kong Indian businessman, hotelier and philanthropist and the founder and chairman of the Harilela Group. The group runs businesses ranging from hotel and real estate investment to import and export trading. He was often dubbed the richest Indian in Hong Kong.

### Rosewood London has promoted Emmanuele Selvaggi to Hotel Manager, effective 16th June 2025. In this elevated role, Emmanuele will continue to drive the hotel’s operational excellence and reinforce Rosewood London’s position as the preferred luxury destination in London.

Emmanuele Selvaggi, General Manager of Rosewood London

Emmanuele brings over 18 years of distinguished experience in luxury hospitality to his new position. He began his career with Four Seasons Boston in the Rooms Division and successfully progressed through the ranks across multiple international locations including Geneva, Doha, Prague and London over 15 years with the prestigious brand. His tenue with Four Seasons culminated as Hotel Manager at Four Seasons Florence before returning to London in November 2021 to join Rosewood London.

linkedin_profile.headline: Global Marketing and PR Director @Starhotels | Forbes Top 100 CMO Italia | Brand Builder | Entrepreneur | Investor | D&I Advocate | Made in Italy Ambassador
linkedin_profile.about: Over the last 15 years, my career has been an incredible journey learning about luxury hospitality, retail, consumer goods, and most importantly people. The differentiating factors for a company are people and innovation. The world is moving fast and great companies have people who challenge convention and lead change. I aim to create an impact, building strategies that break the mold and meet the evolving needs of clients and the global market. I’m a firm believer in the power of motivation, mental well-being, and empathy, I bring a human-centered perspective to my professional endeavors. My passion for creating meaningful experiences and products extends into my interests—whether skiing down mountains, diving, exploring art, or immersing myself in new cultures. Connecting people, brands, and ideas is at the heart of what I do. 🎯 What sets me apart as a CMO? • Versatility & Adaptability : I quickly grasp the unique dynamics in each industry, connecting the dots and bringing fresh perspectives that balance local insights with global strategies. • Impactful Innovation: I’m committed to disruptive innovation with a measurable focus, driving significant ROI and expanding customer bases. • Entrepreneurial and Collaborative Leadership: I read between the line and usually I anticipate market trends and needs, launching initiatives that elevate brands beyond expectations. I also foster an inclusive work environment that champions creativity and team growth. Work ethic is key to me. Ready to connect with like-minded innovators, collaborators, and visionaries who share the commitment to elevate customer experiences and shape the future.

# Maritime Aquarium Welcomes New Harbor Seal In Norwalk

## The Maritime Aquarium in Norwalk has announced the arrival of a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal at the facility.

###### RJ Scofield, Patch Staff

Posted Sun, Apr 26, 2026 at 12:01 pm ET

The Maritime Aquarium in Norwalk announced the arrival of Kaya, a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal, on April 20, 2026. (RJ Scofield/Patch Staff)

NORWALK, CT — The Maritime Aquarium announced this week the arrival of a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal at the Norwalk facility.

According to a news release provided by the aquarium, Kaya arrived in Norwalk earlier this month from the Aquarium of the Pacific in California and is now on exhibit at the Maritime Aquarium's Pinniped Cove exhibit.

Blakes Hotel rescued from administration for £20mdefault-output-block.skip-main

# Blakes Hotel rescued from administration for £20m

By Rachel Johnson

04-Oct-2010 Last updated on 26-Apr-2021 at 21:11 GMT

## Luxury boutique hotel Blakes Hotel in London has been purchased out of administration for around £20 million, as its new owners announce plans to take the brand abroad

Luxury boutique hotel Blakes Hotel in London has been purchased out of administration for around £20million, as its new owners announce plans to take the brand abroad.

The iconic Mayfair hotel was bought by Navid Mirtorabi, hotelier and investor, Meir Abutbul founder of boulanger-patisserie chain Apostrophe.

The partners said they planned to expand the Blakes brand internationally, although nobody was available to comment on the details today.

The 51-room Blakes hotel is set to be redesigned by Anouska Hempel, the same designer who came up with the concept for the hotel when it first opened in 1981.

Meir Abutb

NEWS

The Blakes Hotel in London has been acquired for £20m, and its new owners are considering rolling out the brand across the globe.

The iconic, 52-bedroom hotel was bought out of administration by hoteliers and investors Navid Mirtorabi and Meir Abutbul.

The hotel - designed by Anouska Hempel in 1981 - was acquired by buy-to-let firm GuestInvest in 2007, but that company went into administration in 2008.

Meir Abutbul, who is the director of the new management company Blakes Management Limited, said: “Blakes is spectacular. It looks sumptuous, but we have some improvements in mind and Anouska Hempel will be in charge of design and styling. We are also planning on expanding the Blakes brand internationally.”

linkedin_profile.about: My passion is to find innovative ways to solve business problems and improve operations. By building and growing an IT organization, I ensure the highest level of service to the company and position the business to succeed and grow in the market place. I develop and execute innovative business and technology strategies to support and enhance the mission and business of the organization. I have the knowledge and ability to be a hands-on manager when needed with technical expertise in systems architecture, desktop and server virtualization, network engineering, business intelligence, data warehousing, software engineering and business process improvement. My industry experience includes financial services, e-commerce, retail, higher education, non-profit and performing arts. Specialties · Short and long-term IT Strategic Planning · IT Turnarounds, Team building, and restructuring · Leadership and mentorship of IT professionals · Business Process Improvement · IT Service Management and Operations · IT Governance · Project Management · Data Warehousing and Business Analysis · Vendor management and Outsourcing · eCommerce

linkedin_profile.headline: Director of Public Relations and Communications at Four Seasons Resort Oahu at Ko Olina
linkedin_profile.about: Michelle Edwards is the Director of Public Relations and Communications at Four Seasons Resort Oahu at Ko Olina. A 13-year luxury hospitality veteran, Michelle brings a wealth of public relations expertise, luxury brand know-how and innovation to the project. She is responsible for the development and implementation of the resort’s overall strategic public relations initiatives. Michelle joins Four Seasons Resort Oahu at Ko Olina most recently from Mauna Lani, Auberge Resorts Collection as the Director of Public Relations and was part of the opening resort team. Prior to Mauna Lani, she served as a strategic communications consultant for various Four Seasons properties globally, including Four Seasons Resort Oahu, Four Seasons Hotel Kuala Lumpur, Four Seasons Resort Langkawi, Four Seasons Hotel Doha, Four Seasons Costa Rica, and Four Seasons Hotel Austin. Prior to her consultant role, Edwards was the Director of Public Relations at Four Seasons Resorts Lanai, overseeing public relations for both Manele Bay and The Lodge at Koele. Michelle, who has been at the publicity helm of a myriad of hotel and restaurant openings throughout her career, has garnered global recognition for her innovation, creative vision and flawless execution in developing unique storytelling campaigns and cultivating groundbreaking experiential activations.

experience.title: General Manager - Sydell Group
experience.description: Reporting into the COO and CEO directly, I managed one of New York City’s most successful boutique hotels with 168-luxury rooms, 140-seat Michelin-starred restaurant and the James Beard award winning NoMad bar. Oversaw all aspects of hotel, including finance, sales, human resources, engineering, revenue, guest experience, events, capital expenditure, and budgets. Collaborated with restaurant General Manager to direct food and beverage (F&B). Pre-pandemic, along with my team of department heads, I was responsible for 320 employees in total. Promoted from Hotel Manager to General Manager in 2018. Additional assignments included opening task force for sister properties: Ned London (272 rooms), Line Washington DC (220 rooms), Nomad Las Vegas (293 rooms), Freehand New York (400 rooms) and the upcoming Nomad London (93 rooms). In 2020, closed and reopened Nomad, NY and Line DC due to pandemic and was involved in all strategic planning to get the properties safely and successfully through the pandemic. Some of these strategies in 2020 and into 2021 involved creating new “business models” for the property such as pivoting all F&B outdoors and developing a robust delivery service. Managed re-closure of Nomad New York property due to corporate restructuring in April, 2021.

experience.title: Founder
experience.description: Developed non profit website / organization giving cancer patients and caregivers access to discounted hotel rooms at various partner hotels across New York City, Boston and Miami. These rooms benefit patients by providing safe, comfortable and affordable environments for individuals undergoing treatment as well as create a sense of escapism that can assist the body and mind in the healing process.

# 5 Minutes With… Nicola Wood, Head of Sales at BWH Hotels

- November 25, 2024
- Contact

In the latest instalment of our events industry executive interview series we spoke to Nicola Wood (pictured), Head of Sales at BWH Hotels, about the need for flexibility and personalisation when it comes to meeting rooms, the growing move towards sustainable solutions, the importance of instant online booking systems and more…

Tell us about your company, products and services.

BWH Hotels may seem a new name to many of you, but we have actually been in the UK for 50 plus years. If I was to say Best Western you would all know who we were. The name BWH Hotels was formed after the acquisition of World Hotels. BWH Hotels now have a wide portfolio of global brands and all our properties are independently owned and operated, meaning clients get much more opportunity to personalise their experiences.

Nicola Wood

More than 125,000 Condé Nast Traveller readers across the United Kingdom submitted responses rating their travel experiences across the globe to offer a comprehensive look at the places they eagerly anticipate revisiting. ## Description

Step inside one of London’s most imaginative properties with Patrick Katzenberg, General Manager of The Londoner, as he shares the story behind the world’s first “super boutique” hotel—and what that means for the discerning traveller.

In this episode of “An Audience With…” by Winged Boots, Patrick unpacks how The Londoner merges theatrical flair with quiet luxury, combining grand scale with deeply personal service. From hidden whisky rooms and West End partnerships to immersive design details that honour the hotel’s Leicester Square roots, discover how this standout address is redefining what a city stay can feel like.

What you’ll uncover in this interview:

experience.title: Executive Chef
experience.description: The Market Café is a Modern British Bistro with a large focus on weekend brunches. With a restaurant that can seat up to 110 people, with average dinner covers of around 50-60 and weekend brunch covers of 300-500 people this was an extremely busy kitchen. With a brigade of 7 chefs we managed turn it from a food GP of 36% down to 29% in the space of about four months. With a weekly food turnover now of £15,000-£20,000 it ha shown an increase of 20% from the same times in 2014-2015. Les Nenettes is a small French Bistro, opened in February, located in Hackney open all day serving Brunch, Lunch and Dinner. With seating of 40 inside and 25 outside with an extremely small kitchen we have got this up and running in under 3 months. A brigade of 5 chefs and 2 KPs. As Executive Chef I was responsible for the day to day running of both restaurants and more recently, designing and opening the Les Nenettes Restaurant.

experience.title: Head Chef
experience.description: Eat 17 Restaurant is a neighbourhood bistro with a large clientele of regular diners. With seating of around 70 covers Eat 17 does on average around 1300 covers a week. Open for lunch and dinner Monday-Friday, and brunch, lunch and dinner on the weekends, it is a fast paced restaurant while still maintaining its high standards in food and service. As the Head Chef my responsibilities include, rota, wage costings, menu design and implementation, menu costings and all aspects of health and safety.

experience.title: Executive Chef
experience.description: The Oslo Bar and Restaurant is a busy bar/restaurant/live music venue. It is based in Hackney, East London and is a joint restaurant/bar and live music venue. I was employed as the Executive Chef to oversee the complete overhaul of the Oslo kitchen. In early July we created a new plan and direction for the Oslo kitchen. After a reorganisation of the kitchen and surrounding areas to improve efficiency, we focused on staff training and taking the food back to basics with 90% of the items being made in house. With regular food training, on tasting, allergens, coffee training, wine training and an in house 10 module health, hygiene and safety program we re-launched the Oslo kitchen. The restaurant has a maximum seating of 80pax and is open for lunch and dinner 7 days a week. With a focus on fresh food, using local suppliers where possible, such as the E5 Bakehouse, Fin and Flounder & Cream Lab. The restaurant is marketed as an inviting gastro pub, serving fresh, homemade comfort food. With daily changing specials, a very busy Sunday lunch and a packed restaurant on music nights, the Oslo has transformed into a staple East London dining choice.

# BEST CONFERENCE VENUE

#### 116 Pall Mall

116 Pall Mall is a Grade I listed Georgian building opened in 1828, serving as home to the Institute of Directors and one of London’s premier conference venues. Located on prestigious Pall Mall, it combines distinguished heritage with modern event capabilities delivered through warm Searcys hospitality.

Post-COVID transformation saw remarkable growth from 15 to 80 staff across seven departments, achieving 67% revenue increase from £4.5 million to £7.5 million. The venue accommodates 12 to 350 delegates, featuring the versatile Nash Room with stunning period décor and modern technical capabilities.

Our competitive advantage stems from central location, comprehensive service delivery, and holistic approach combining venue hire, catering, technology, and event management. Key innovations include hybrid event technology, ESG strategy with 20% waste reduction ta

About us — 116 Pall Mall

# About us

## Introducing 116 Pall Mall

A special event calls for an extraordinary venue.

A stunning example of Georgian architecture and elegance, 116 Pall Mall is a Grade I listed building presiding over one of London’s most prestigious addresses. Opened in 1828, it’s now home to the Institute of Directors.

116 Pall Mall also plays host to hundreds of corporate occasions, private events, festive celebrations and weddings every year, bringing a sense of occasion to even the most intimate gatherings. Guests are instantly captivated by the building’s luxurious interiors, complete with magnificent period features alongside its unique heritage and the impeccable service provided by our team.

We offer a wide range of versatile and flexible spaces throughout our five floors, welcoming up to 1,500 guests, and our enviably central location makes travel to your event a breeze.

The collection includes four main properties: Montcalm East in Shoreditch, Montcalm Marble Arch in Marylebone, Montcalm Royal London House in Finsbury Square, and Montcalm Brewery in Barbican. Each hotel within the Montcalm Collection has its own unique story and design, blending classic and contemporary elements to reﬂect the character of London. Montcalm Brewery is a historic former brewery transformed into a ﬁve-star hotel, while Montcalm Royal London House is a mid-century marvel with a stunning rooftop terrace. The hotels are known for their thoughtful touches, luxurious comfort, and exceptional personal service. Montcalm East is an Autograph Collection hotel with a mission to support its vibrant neighbourhood of East London. In addition to the Montcalm hotels, the collection also includes the: Montcalm Townhouses, Inhabit Hotels (member of Design Hotels) and the Park Grand Hotels. Inhabit Hotels are urban wellness properties in West London. Wellbeing is at the heart of what we do, as we offer guests both restorative and invigorating experiences to foster mindfulness, healthy habits, and even better sleep. We are committed to enhancing green initiatives by monitoring and reducing overall consumption levels and converting environmental efforts into sustainable business opportunities. Social benchmarks are likewise core to our strategic decision-making. Inhabit Hotels have been awarded B Corp™ Certification, reﬂecting our commitment to social and environmental sustainability. Park Grand Hotels are contemporary boutique hotels located in prime locations throughout London. Montcalm Collection provides a range of luxury accommodations in London. linkedin_profile.headline: Vice President Commercial at The Red Carnation Hotel Collection (Red Carnation Hotels)
linkedin_profile.about:
With a robust leadership role at Red Carnation Hotels, my focus is on shaping global commercial strategies for an exquisite collection of luxury boutique hotels. The heart of my role lies in managing and strategically developing sales, marketing, revenue, distribution, and reservations teams. Success in these areas has been marked by the launch of four new properties, creating a central reservations office, leading significant technology projects such as Opera Cloud PMS, Salesforce CRM and a guest service app, whilst showcasing a commitment to sustainable luxury in the hospitality industry. Collaborating closely with strategic agency partnerships, we have optimized revenue growth opportunities across 18 properties worldwide. My approach is both creative and data-driven, ensuring that every decision aligns with our mission to deliver unparalleled customer experiences. This includes a keen emphasis on customer acquisition, conversion, retention, and cross/up-selling, all while fostering authentic, purpose-driven initiatives that support sustainable travel.

Total Experience: 23 years

Managing Director at Claridge's

Thomas Kochs: The Visionary Luxury Hotel Leader Behind Claridge’s Global Prestige - News Dip

Thomas Kochs

Thomas Kochs is one of the most respected figures in the global luxury hospitality industry, known for his impeccable leadership, refined operational vision, and long-standing contribution to world-class hotel experiences. His name has become synonymous with elegance, precision, and the highest standards of service excellence. Rising from an apprenticeship in Germany to becoming Managing Director at some of London’s most iconic luxury hotels, Kochs’ journey reflects commitment, discipline, and passion for hospitality at its finest.

Today, Thomas Kochs serves as the Managing Director at Claridge’s, one of the most prestigious hotels in the world. His previous leadership roles at Corinthia London, Hotel Café Royal, and The Connaught further define his distinguished career.

# Thomas Kochs Appointed General Manager of Claridge’s in London

| Travel News Asia | Latest Travel News | Podcasts | Videos | Friday, 14 January 2011 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

Maybourne Hotel Group, the owner and operator of London’s renowned luxury hotels Claridge’s, The Connaught and The Berkeley, has appointed Thomas Kochs as General Manager of Claridge’s, the iconic Mayfair landmark.

Kochs, who has worked in all three Maybourne properties since joining the company in 2001, most recently held the position of Hotel Manager at Claridge’s. In this role he successfully oversaw projects such as the creation and launch of the Diane von Furstenberg rooms and suites as well as ending 2010 with the best financial results in the hotel’s recent history.

Tim Rumney

# Welcome

Like many people I fell into the hotel industry by accident after taking a holiday job. Following a summer spent working and playing hard, I had a decision to make about whether this was going to be my career or if I was going to follow my father into journalism. The hotel won and I spent the next two years as a trainee manager working in all departments of the hotel. After attending university and blagging a placement at the Capital Hotel in Knightsbridge, I moved on to the Forte Hotel Group. The rest as they say, is history.

The boss of a York-based hotel franchise has warned many hotels are stopping recruitment, investments and will hike prices.

Tim Rumney, CEO of BWH Hotels GB, says more than four-fifths of the 230 hotels in the chain are already cutting staff hours to cope with rising costs.

Mr Rumney called it encouraging inflation had dropped to 2.8 per cent in February but said the Chancellor’s Spring Statement “does little to ease the pressures on independent hoteliers.”
